content:
# 1 Corinthians Chapter 1 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/1CO_001_page_001.jpg

1. Paulos, called apostle of Christos Yeshua through [the] will of God, and Sosthenes the brother,

2. to the assembly of God, the [one] being in Korinthos, having been sanctified in Christos Yeshua, called holy [ones], with all the [ones] calling upon the name of the Lord of us Yeshua Christos in every place, theirs and ours:

3. Grace to you and peace from God [the] Father of us and [the] Lord Yeshua Christos.

4. I give thanks to the God of me always concerning you, upon the grace of God, the [one] having been given to you in Christos Yeshua,

5. that in everything you were enriched in him, in all speech and all knowledge,

6. just as the testimony of the Christos was confirmed in you,

7. so that you are not lacking in any gift, awaiting the revelation of the Lord of us Yeshua Christos,

8. who also will confirm you until [the] end, blameless in the day of the Lord of us Yeshua Christos.

9. Faithful [is] God, through whom you were called into [the] fellowship of the Son of him, Yeshua Christos the Lord of us.

10. And I urge you, brothers, through the name of the Lord of us Yeshua Christos, that you all say the same [thing], and there be not among you divisions, but you be restored in the same mind and in the same purpose.

11. For it was made clear to me concerning you, brothers of me, by the [ones] of Chloe, that quarrels exist among you.

12. And I say this, that each of you says, "I am of Paulos," and "I of Apollos," and "I of Kephas," and "I of Christos."

13. Has the Christos been divided? Was Paulos crucified for you? Or were you baptized into the name of Paulos?

14. I give thanks that I baptized none of you except Krispos and Gaios,

15. lest anyone should say that you were baptized into the name of me.

16. And I baptized also the household of Stephanas; beyond that, I do not know whether I baptized any other.

17. For Christos did not send me to baptize but to proclaim good news, not in wisdom of speech, lest the cross of the Christos be emptied.

18. For the word of the cross is foolishness to the [ones] perishing, but to us the [ones] being saved it is [the] power of God.

19. For it has been written, "I will destroy the wisdom of the wise, and the understanding of the understanding [ones] I will set aside."

20. Where [is the] wise? Where [is the] scribe? Where [is the] debater of this age? Did not God make foolish the wisdom of the world?

21. For since in the wisdom of God the world did not know God through the wisdom, God was pleased through the foolishness of the proclamation to save the [ones] believing.

22. Since indeed Ioudaioi ask for signs and Greeks seek wisdom,

23. but we proclaim Christos crucified — to Ioudaioi a stumbling block and to nations foolishness,

24. but to the [ones] called, both Ioudaioi and Greeks, Christos [the] power of God and [the] wisdom of God.

25. Because the foolishness of God is wiser than the men, and the weakness of God is stronger than the men.

26. For look at the calling of you, brothers, that not many wise according to flesh, not many powerful, not many well-born;

27. but the foolish [things] of the world God chose, that he might shame the wise, and the weak [things] of the world God chose, that he might shame the strong,

28. and the low-born of the world and the [ones] having been despised God chose, the [things] not being, that he might bring to nothing the [things] being,

29. so that all flesh might not boast before God.

30. But from him you are in Christos Yeshua, who became wisdom to us from God, and righteousness and sanctification and redemption,

31. that, just as it has been written, "The [one] boasting, let him boast in [the] Lord."

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.1 — Sosthenes:** Possibly the synagogue ruler of Acts 18:17, now a Christian co-sender. Paul names a co-author but the letter is singularly Pauline in voice.

2. **v.2 — "with all who call upon the name":** The letter is addressed to Corinth but has a universal horizon. This may explain its early and wide circulation.

3. **v.12 — The four slogans:** Παύλου, Ἀπολλώ, Κηφᾶ, Χριστοῦ. The Christos-party slogan is the most debated — are they a real faction or Paul's rhetorical climax showing the absurdity of all party claims?

4. **v.14 — Krispos:** Likely the synagogue ruler of Acts 18:8. Sinaiticus reads Κρίσπον clearly.

5. **v.17 — "not in wisdom of speech" (οὐκ ἐν σοφίᾳ λόγου):** This phrase introduces the great wisdom/foolishness dialectic that governs chapters 1-4. σοφία appears 17 times in chapters 1-3.

6. **v.22 — "Ioudaioi ask for signs":** σημεῖα — the same word used in the Gospels for Jesus's miraculous works. Greeks seek σοφίαν — philosophical wisdom. Paul's gospel satisfies neither expectation.

— EMET, AI Translator
